questions.text: Why does the Surah mention the sky cleaving, stars scattering, seas bursting, and graves overturning?
questions.answer: To show the end of worldly order and the beginning of full exposure of deeds.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that the unseen record of deeds will stay hidden forever.
questions.answer: False. It teaches deeds will be exposed and known.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about a soul knowing what it sent ahead and left behind?
questions.answer: A person will realize both their actions and their neglected responsibilities when accountability arrives.

questions.text: How does calling Allah 'Al-Karim' (The Most Generous) shame the sinner?
questions.answer: It highlights that rebellion is against a Lord who gave many gifts, making ingratitude more blameworthy.

questions.text: What is one reason people become ‘deluded’ about Allah according to the Surah’s message?
questions.answer: They mistake Allah’s patience and worldly ease for approval and forget the coming reckoning.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests that Allah’s generosity means there will be no judgment.
questions.answer: False. It combines Allah’s generosity with firm accountability.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Allah’s role in shaping the human body?
questions.answer: Allah created, proportioned, balanced, and assembled humans in forms He willed.

questions.text: What is one lesson from Allah proportioning and balancing the human?
questions.answer: Human life is designed with wisdom; arrogance and denial contradict the reality of being created.

questions.text: What does Surah Al-Infitar teach about the recording angels (Kiraman Katibin)?
questions.answer: They are noble scribes appointed to record deeds accurately.

questions.text: How can awareness of recording angels improve daily behavior?
questions.answer: It increases self-control, honesty, and sincerity even when no humans are watching.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that private sins are less important because they are unseen by people.
questions.answer: False. Private deeds are recorded and will be shown.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the final split between Abrar and Fujjar?
questions.answer: The righteous enter نعيم, and the wicked enter جحيم; outcomes match the life choices.

questions.text: What is one practical difference between Abrar and Fujjar in daily life?
questions.answer: Abrar are truthful and God-conscious, while Fujjar persist in sin and denial.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach individual responsibility on Judgment Day?
questions.answer: No soul can help another; authority and judgment belong to Allah alone.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that family and friends will solve your accountability problems.
questions.answer: False. No soul possesses power to help another on that Day.

questions.text: What does Surah Al-Infitar teach about people who deny the Day of Recompense?
questions.answer: Denial is the root reason for moral collapse and leads to punishment.

questions.text: How can a believer use this Surah to fight spiritual procrastination?
questions.answer: By remembering the sudden upheaval and exposure of deeds, then repenting and correcting one habit immediately.

questions.text: How does the Surah connect gratitude to belief in accountability?
questions.answer: Gratitude recognizes Allah’s generosity and leads to obedience; denial leads to ingratitude and rebellion.

questions.text: What is one daily practice to strengthen gratitude based on this Surah?
questions.answer: Name three blessings each day and respond with one act of obedience (prayer, charity, honesty).

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that shame and guilt are always negative emotions.
questions.answer: False. Proper shame can awaken repentance when directed toward Allah’s generosity and our failures.

questions.text: What does 'Al-Infitar' mean?
questions.answer: The Cleaving or Splitting asunder of the sky.

questions.text: What will happen to the planets/stars (Al-Kawakib)?
questions.answer: They will scatter and fall.

questions.text: What will happen to the seas (Al-Bihar)?
questions.answer: They will be erupted or made to burst forth.

questions.text: What will happen to the graves (Al-Qubur)?
questions.answer: They will be overturned or ransacked.

questions.text: What is the question Allah asks to the ungrateful human in this Surah?
questions.answer: What has deluded you concerning your Lord, the Generous?

questions.text: What does this Surah mention Allah did for the human after creating him?
questions.answer: He proportioned him and balanced him.

questions.text: How did Allah choose the human form?
questions.answer: He assembled the human in whatever form He willed.

questions.text: What is the core reason people deny the judgment according to this Surah?
questions.answer: They deny the Recompense (the Day of Judgment).

questions.text: Who are the 'Kiraman Katibin'?
questions.answer: Noble recording angels appointed over every human.

questions.text: Do the recording angels know what we do?
questions.answer: Yes, they know whatever you do.

questions.text: Where will the 'Abrar' (the righteous) be?
questions.answer: In 'Na'im' (Pleasure/Paradise).

questions.text: Where will the 'Fujjar' (the wicked) be?
questions.answer: In 'Jahim' (Hellfire).

questions.text: When will the wicked enter the Fire?
questions.answer: On the Day of Recompense.

questions.text: Can the wicked ever leave the Hellfire once they enter?
questions.answer: No, they will not be absent from it.

questions.text: What is the 'Day of Recompense'?
questions.answer: The day when every soul is rewarded or punished for its deeds.

questions.text: Can one soul benefit another on that Day?
questions.answer: No, no soul will possess power to help another soul at all.

questions.text: To whom belongs the command on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: The command entirely belongs to Allah.

questions.text: How does this Surah describe Allah to evoke shame in the sinner?
questions.answer: Al-Karim (The Most Generous).

questions.text: What is 'Ad-Din' in the context of this Surah?
questions.answer: It refers to the Reckoning or the Recompense.

questions.text: Does this Surah emphasize individual responsibility?
questions.answer: Yes, by highlighting that records are kept and no one can assist another on that Day.
